content.description: A relative clause connects two ideas about the same person, place or thing without writing two separate sentences. We introduce these clauses with a relative pronoun.

content.subtitle: Use
items: who → refers to people: The student **who** got the highest mark received a prize.
which → refers to things and animals: The jacket **which** I bought on sale is already broken.
where → refers to places: That's the park **where** we used to play as children.
whose → shows who something belongs to: I met a woman **whose** son plays in a famous band.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: We can use who for a pet or animal when we treat it as a personality: My cat, who's called Luna, sleeps all day.
After the relative pronoun, do not repeat the subject or object pronoun.
✓ She's the woman **who** designed this building.
✓ Here's the report **which** you asked for.

content.description: A non-defining relative clause adds bonus information about someone or something. The main sentence is still completely clear and correct without it.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To add extra detail that is not essential to identifying the noun: My grandfather, **who** turned 80 last year, still goes jogging every morning.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: Non-defining relative clauses are always separated from the main clause by commas.
Our history teacher, **whose** books are always fascinating, won an award last month.

content.description:
A defining relative clause identifies exactly which person, thing or place we are talking about. Without it, the sentence loses its meaning or becomes unclear.
Imagine there are several students in a classroom. Only one of them has a red backpack.
*The student **who** has the red backpack is my best friend.*
If we take out the relative clause, we have no idea which student is meant.

items: ✗ The student is my best friend.

content.subtitle: Use
items: To specify which person or thing we mean: The phone **which** rang during the lesson belonged to a student.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: We do not use commas with defining relative clauses.
In defining relative clauses, *that* can replace *who* or *which*.
✓ The doctor *who* treated me was very kind.
✓ The doctor *that* treated me was very kind.

sections.title: A: Complete with a relative pronoun
sections.instructions: Write who, which, where or whose in each gap.

items.sentence: Do you remember the man ...... helped us at the station?
This is the camera ...... I used on holiday.
That's the library ...... I study after school.
I have a friend ...... mother works at the hospital.
The doctor ...... examined me was very friendly.
Is this the song ...... you heard on the radio?
They stayed in a hotel ...... the rooms had sea views.
The girl ...... bag was lost looked very upset.

sections.title: B: Defining or non-defining
sections.instructions: Add commas where needed. If no commas are needed, write 'No commas'.

items.sentence: My brother who lives in Manchester is a chef.
items.answer: My brother, who lives in Manchester, is a chef.

items.sentence: The bike which is outside the shop is mine.
Mrs Carter who teaches history is retiring soon.
items.answer: Mrs Carter, who teaches history, is retiring soon.

items.sentence: The people who arrived late missed the introduction.
This castle which was built in the 12th century is open to visitors.
items.answer: This castle, which was built in the 12th century, is open to visitors.

items.sentence: Students who forget their homework must speak to the teacher.

sections.title: C: Use who, which or that
sections.instructions: Choose the best option in each sentence.

items.sentence: He's the neighbour **who / which / where** looks after our dog.
This is the programme **who / that / where** everyone watched last night.
Do you know a restaurant **that / who / whose** serves vegetarian food?
The man **which / whose / where** wallet was stolen called the police.
That's the village **who / where / whose** my dad grew up.
The tablet **who / which / whose** I ordered arrived yesterday.
She's the singer **which / that / where** won the competition.
We visited the gallery **where / whose / which** has modern paintings.

sections.title: D: Join the sentences
sections.instructions: Rewrite each pair as one sentence using a relative clause.

items.sentence: I know a boy. He can solve a Rubik's cube very quickly.
items.answer: I know a boy who can solve a Rubik's cube very quickly.

items.sentence: We rented a flat. It was close to the station.
items.answer: We rented a flat which was close to the station.

items.sentence: That's the girl. Her sister won the prize.
items.answer: That's the girl whose sister won the prize.

items.sentence: This is the school. My mother studied there.
items.answer: This is the school where my mother studied.

items.sentence: I found a wallet. It was under the table.
items.answer: I found a wallet which was under the table.

items.sentence: The café is very popular. We met there on Saturday.
items.answer: The café where we met on Saturday is very popular.

sections.title: E: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake with relative clauses. Find the error and write the whole sentence correctly.

items.sentence: That's the girl who **she** won the art prize.
items.answer: That's the girl who won the art prize.

items.sentence: This is the bag which I bought **it** yesterday.
items.answer: This is the bag which I bought yesterday.

items.sentence: My cousin, who **he** works in Berlin, is coming home.
items.answer: My cousin, who works in Berlin, is coming home.

items.sentence: The film **who** we watched was really funny.
items.answer: The film which we watched was really funny.

items.sentence: The house where I lived **there** is near the river.
items.answer: The house where I lived is near the river.

items.sentence: My neighbour **that he** plays the drums is very noisy.
items.answer: My neighbour who plays the drums is very noisy.

sections.title: F: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word in bold.

items.sentence:
The woman is a doctor. Her daughter is in my class. **WHOSE**
The woman ...... daughter is in my class is a doctor.

This is the shop. I bought my jacket there. **WHERE**
This is the shop ...... I bought my jacket.

I found a phone. It belongs to Sam. **WHICH**
I found a phone ...... belongs to Sam.

The man fixed our car. I spoke to him yesterday. **WHO**
The man ...... I spoke to yesterday fixed our car.

Do you know the reason? They cancelled the trip for that reason. **WHY**
Do you know the reason ...... they cancelled the trip?

This is the beach. We learned to surf here. **WHERE**
This is the beach ...... we learned to surf.
The woman ...... opened the door was my aunt.
That's the dog ...... owner lives next door.
This is the café ...... we met Liam.
The jacket ...... I bought yesterday is too small.
Do you know the boy ...... father is a firefighter?
The town ...... we stayed was very quiet.
She's the player ...... scored the winning goal.
The headphones ...... I want are on sale.

sections.title: H: Complete the text
sections.instructions: Write one relative word in each gap.

items.sentence: I know a teacher ...... works in Edinburgh.
She teaches at a school ...... has students from many countries.
The school, ...... is near the city centre, is very old.
Her colleague is a man ...... lessons are always exciting.
The classroom ...... they study has a large screen.
She explained the reason ...... she became a teacher.
She has students ...... speak more than two languages.
It's the kind of school ...... I would love to visit.

sections.title: I: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
I know a musician. She plays in an orchestra.
I know a musician ...... in an orchestra.

The scarf was beautiful. I bought it at the market.
The scarf ...... at the market was beautiful.

There's a shop near here. It sells handmade gifts.
There's a shop ...... handmade gifts near here.

She is a painter. Her latest picture won a prize.
She is a painter ...... a prize.

The village was peaceful. We visited it in June.
The village ...... in June was peaceful.

He works with a doctor. She studies tropical diseases.
He works with a doctor ...... tropical diseases.
